name: Magic Resource, Execution, and Resolution Model

resource_architecture.overcasting: Higher-tier capacity may power lower-tier effects, but substitution raises visibility, strain, and enforcement risk even when the effect technically succeeds.

execution_channels.voice: spoken patterns, chants, legal formulae, names, prayers, command words
execution_channels.gesture: trained body-memory, sign sequence, circle work, loom or seal motions
execution_channels.material: inks, fibers, metals, glass, relic fragments, catalysts, sanctified or consumed media
execution_channels.spatial: correct relation to target, threshold, orientation, chamber, or sanctified surface
execution_channels.witness: some rites require hearing, countersign, recording, identity confirmation, or public certification

persistence.rule: Persistent effects always place burden somewhere: on concentration, on a prepared site, on a relic, on institutional maintenance, or on the target itself.
persistence.trace_rule: The longer an effect persists, the more likely it is to leave residue, archive evidence, sanctity shifts, or detectable trace.
